perturbation.original_text: “ Affiliate ” for purposes of this Agreement shall mean any one or more business entities which are: (a) owned or controlled by, (b) owning or controlling, or (c) under common control with a Party at the time in question. Ownership, direct or indirect, of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting stock or other equity interests of an entity ordinarily entitled to vote in the election of directors or similar governing body of an entity shall, without limitation, constitute ownership or control thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: “ Affiliate ” for purposes of this Agreement shall mean any one or more business entities which are: (a) owned or controlled by, (b) owning or controlling, or (c) under common control with a Party at the time in question. Ownership, direct or indirect, of more than twenty-five percent (25%) of the voting stock or other equity interests of an entity ordinarily entitled to vote in the election of directors or similar governing body of an entity shall, without limitation, constitute ownership or control thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Affiliate' is changed from 50% ownership to 25% ownership. This in-text contradiction impacts Section 9, which refers to 'Affiliates' regarding standstill restrictions, because now a company with 25% ownership is subject to the same restrictions. This creates uncertainty and potential legal disputes regarding the scope and enforcement of the standstill agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the one-year period commencing on the date of this Agreement (the “Standstill Period”), neither the Company nor any of its Representatives will, in any manner, directly or indirectly (except to the extent agreed by Verenium): (a) make, effect, initiate, cause or participate in (i) any acquisition of beneficial ownership of any securities of Verenium or any securities of any subsidiary or other Affiliate of Verenium, in excess of five (5) percent of the outstanding securities of any such company (with any such acquisition, regardless of size, only being made at such time as neither the acquiring party nor any of its Affiliates is in possession of material, non-public information about Verenium), (ii) any acquisition of any assets of Verenium or any assets of any subsidiary or other Affiliate of Verenium, (iii) any tender offer, exchange offer, merger, business combination, recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or extraordinary transaction involving Verenium or any subsidiary or other Affiliate of Verenium, or involving any securities or assets of Verenium or any securities or assets of any subsidiary or other Affiliate of Verenium, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as those terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents with respect to any securities of Verenium; (b) form, join or participate in a “group” (as defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and the rules promulgated thereunder) with respect to the beneficial ownership of any securities of Verenium; (c) act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control or influence the management, board of directors or policies of Verenium; (d) take any action that might require Verenium to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in clause “(a)” of this sentence; (e) agree or offer to take, or encourage or propose (publicly or otherwise) the taking of, any action referred to in clause “(a)”, “(b)”, “(c)” or “(d)” of this sentence; (f) assist, induce or encourage any other person or entity to take any action of the type referred to in clause “(a)”, “(b)”, “(c)”, “(d)” or “(e)” of this sentence; or (g) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangement or agreement with any other person or entity relating to any of the foregoing. The expiration of the Standstill Period will not terminate or otherwise affect any of the other provisions of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (i) the Company and its Representatives shall not be precluded from submitting a topping or other offer or otherwise participating in any process not initiated by the Company or its Affiliates or Representatives in which the securities or assets of Verenium or any subsidiary or other Affiliate of Verenium are for sale or are being or have been sold, and (ii) upon filing for protection under US Bankruptcy laws by Verenium or any subsidiary or Affiliate of Verenium the restrictions of this paragraph 9 shall be void and of no further force or effect with respect to the filing entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Affiliate' from 50% ownership to 25% ownership. The contradicted text, Section 9, imposes standstill restrictions on the Company and its Affiliates. Because the definition of affiliate is modified, this impacts the scope and enforcement of the standstill agreement. A company with 25% ownership is now subject to the same restrictions as a company with 50% ownership. This creates a clear inconsistency as it broadens the scope of entities affected by the standstill agreement in Section 9, directly contradicting the original intent.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees to treat Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium, with at least the same degree of care that Company uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees to treat Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium, with at least a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'the same degree of care that Company uses to protect its own Confidential Information' is replaced with 'a reasonable degree of care.' This introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 3 previously stated that Company would protect Verenium's information with the same rigor as its own. The change creates ambiguity about the required level of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company agrees to treat Confidential Information received from or on behalf of Verenium, with at least a reasonable degree of care. Company shall only disseminate Confidential Information to such of its Representatives with a special need to know for carrying out the Purpose of this Agreement. In addition, Company hereby agrees that such Representatives shall be informed of the confidential and proprietary nature of the Confidential Information received and that such Representatives shall be bound by the terms hereof or subject to confidentiality and non-use obligations no less restrictive than those set forth herein. Company shall be liable for any disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives in a manner not authorized by this Agreement. “ Representative ” means any employee, consultant, contractor, officer, agent, advisor, and/or director of a Party. “ Affiliate ” for purposes of this Agreement shall mean any one or more business entities which are: (a) owned or controlled by, (b) owning or controlling, or (c) under common control with a Party at the time in question. Ownership, direct or indirect, of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting stock or other equity interests of an entity ordinarily entitled to vote in the election of directors or similar governing body of an entity shall, without limitation, constitute ownership or control thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Company will treat Verenium's confidential information with 'the same degree of care that Company uses to protect its own Confidential Information'. The changed text modifies this to 'a reasonable degree of care'. This change directly contradicts the contradicted text, which is the same as the Changed text, but section 3 previously stated that the Company would protect Verenium's information with the same rigor as its own. The change introduces ambiguity about the required level of protection, thus creating an inconsistency and an in-text contradiction as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate one (1) year thereafter, unless earlier terminated upon 30 days written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that the duties set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 shall survive for the 2 periods set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 and the Company’s duties and obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed during the term (or any extension) of this Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate one (1) year thereafter, unless earlier terminated upon 30 days written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that the duties set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 shall survive for the 2 periods set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 and the Company’s duties and obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed during the term (or any extension) of this Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of two (2) years.
perturbation.explanation: The survival period for protecting confidential information after termination is changed from five years to two years. This in-text contradiction directly conflicts with the last sentence of Section 12, creating uncertainty regarding the actual duration of confidentiality obligations after the agreement ends. It undermines the strength of the original confidentiality commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate one (1) year thereafter, unless earlier terminated upon 30 days written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that the duties set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 shall survive for the 2 periods set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 and the Company’s duties and obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed during the term (or any extension) of this Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of two (2) years. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company’s duties and obligations to protect a trade secret disclosed hereunder shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a five-year survival period for confidentiality obligations, while the changed text reduces this period to two years. The contradicted text then states that Company’s duties and obligations to protect a trade secret disclosed hereunder shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement indefinitely. The modified survival period directly contradicts this indefinite survival period as well as the original 5 year survival period.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company’s duties and obligations to protect a trade secret disclosed hereunder shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement indefinitely.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company’s duties and obligations to protect a trade secret disclosed hereunder shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of ten (10) years.
perturbation.explanation: The protection period for trade secrets, originally indefinite, is now limited to ten years. This introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 12, as it creates a time limit for trade secret protection that was previously unlimited. This directly weakens the protection afforded to trade secrets and creates ambiguity regarding their long-term security.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall terminate one (1) year thereafter, unless earlier terminated upon 30 days written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that the duties set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 shall survive for the 2 periods set forth in paragraphs 9 and 13 and the Company’s duties and obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed during the term (or any extension) of this Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of two (2) years. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Company’s duties and obligations to protect a trade secret disclosed hereunder shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of ten (10) years.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Company's duties and obligations to protect a trade secret survive the termination of the Agreement indefinitely. The changed text limits this survival to a period of ten (10) years. The Contradicted Text shows the original text included as part of a larger section of the original agreement. This creates a direct conflict between the 'indefinite' protection in the original text and the limited 'ten (10) years' protection in the changed text, which is a clear inconsistency and would cause disputes over contractual obligations based on Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction and the 5th definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 13. During the one-year period commencing on the date of this Agreement (the “Non-Solicitation Period”), Company shall not, nor shall it permit its Affiliates or Representatives, to: (a) hire any employee of Verenium or enter into a contract with any employee of Verenium to provide services to Company (or any of its Affiliates), in each case involving an employee of Verenium with whom Company has contact as a result of this Agreement or discussions relating to a possible transaction between the Parties, without obtaining the prior written approval of Verenium, or (b) directly or indirectly, induce or attempt to induce or otherwise solicit, counsel, discuss, advise or encourage any such employee to leave or otherwise terminate such employee’s relationship with Verenium; provided, however, that the foregoing provisions of this paragraph 13 shall not apply to any employee of Verenium who was previously terminated by Verenium or who responds to a general solicitation or advertisement regarding employment with Company or its Affiliates. The expiration of the Non-Solicitation Period will not terminate or otherwise affect any of the other provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. During the one-year period commencing on the date of this Agreement (the “Non-Solicitation Period”), Company shall not, nor shall it permit its Affiliates or Representatives, to: (a) hire any employee of Verenium or enter into a contract with any employee of Verenium to provide services to Company (or any of its Affiliates), in each case involving an employee of Verenium with whom Company has contact as a result of this Agreement or discussions relating to a possible transaction between the Parties, without obtaining the prior written approval of Verenium, or (b) directly or indirectly, induce or attempt to induce or otherwise solicit, counsel, discuss, advise or encourage any such employee to leave or otherwise terminate such employee’s relationship with Verenium. The expiration of the Non-Solicitation Period will not terminate or otherwise affect any of the other provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'provided, however, that the foregoing provisions of this paragraph 13 shall not apply to any employee of Verenium who was previously terminated by Verenium or who responds to a general solicitation or advertisement regarding employment with Company or its Affiliates', the text introduces an in-text contradiction by making the non-solicitation agreement all encompassing. Section 13 is now fully contradicted since the agreement had clauses that were to consider when not terminating an employee, now all clauses have been removed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the one-year period commencing on the date of this Agreement (the “Non-Solicitation Period”), Company shall not, nor shall it permit its Affiliates or Representatives, to: (a) hire any employee of Verenium or enter into a contract with any employee of Verenium to provide services to Company (or any of its Affiliates), in each case involving an employee of Verenium with whom Company has contact as a result of this Agreement or discussions relating to a possible transaction between the Parties, without obtaining the prior written approval of Verenium, or (b) directly or indirectly, induce or attempt to induce or otherwise solicit, counsel, discuss, advise or encourage any such employee to leave or otherwise terminate such employee’s relationship with Verenium. The expiration of the Non-Solicitation Period will not terminate or otherwise affect any of the other provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 13 removes the exceptions to the non-solicitation agreement. The original text had a clause that stated that the non-solicitation agreement did not apply to employees who were previously terminated or responded to a general solicitation. By removing this clause, the non-solicitation agreement becomes all-encompassing, which contradicts the original intent and creates a strong inconsistency. The 'Contradicted Text' is the original Section 13. Now the new section 13 directly contradicts the original section 13.
